rulings.Case Title: JULIA HARRIS et al VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B/P)AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Granting Leave To File First Amended Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, August 11, 2009, line 1, PLAINTIFF JULIA HARRIS, CARLA PARK, KEVIN HARRIS, CLARICA HARRIS, and, GABRIELLE HARRIS Motion For Order Granting Leave To File First Amended Complaint. Granted. No opposition filed. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: MICHAEL PRINCE VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B*P)AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION /Amended To Dismiss For Failure To Serve Complaint Within Two Years (Change Of Hearing Date)
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, August 11, 2009, line 2, DEFENDANT PEP BOYS MANNY MOE & JACK OF CALIFORNIA MOTION To Dismiss For Failure To Serve Complaint Within Two Years. Motion denied. Improper motion for reconsideration (CCP section 1008). To the extent the motion is based on improper use of the Doe procedure, that is not a basis for a motion to dismiss absent a showing that the claims against the defendant are time-barred. Request for sanctions denied. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: 1884 LLC A CALIFORNIA LIMITED LIABILITY VS. LOUIS ROESCH COMPANY A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Continued Hearing On Motion For Attorneys' Fees
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, August 11, 2009, line 3, DEFENDANT LOUIS ROESCH COMPANY Notice Of Continued Hearing On Motion For Attorneys' Fees. Motion for attorneys' fees granted, no opposition filed. Defendant awarded attorneys' fees in the amount of $181,578. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: KENNETH M. WESLEY VS. ART GALLAGER et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, August 11, 2009, line 4, DEFENDANT DANIEL BERKO MOTION TO STRIKE FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT. Motion denied. The complaint arises from defendant's constitutionally-protected activity. However, plaintiff establishes a probability that he will prevail in his malicious prosecution claims. The Contra Costa County law suit was filed by defendant Berko on behalf of defendant Gallagher. Defendant only prevailed on the assault and battery cause of action and did not prevail on the remaining claims which are severable, and form the basis of this malicious prosecution case. (See Paramount General Hospital Co. vs. Jay (1989) 213 Cal.App. 3d 360). The judgment in the Contra Costa County case on the declaratory relief action was against Gallagher, thus there is probable cause that the proceeding was instituted primarily for an improper purpose. Malice may be established by the rejection of the CCP section 998 offer. Furthermore, the lack of written evidence of Gallagher's alleged ownership in the property, in light of the clear legal requirement that such writing be produced, suggests an inference of malicious intent. Plaintiff was damaged by having to defend himself in the former lawsuit and by his inability to sell the house. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: GARY E GROTE et al VS. DAVID DRIVER et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Amended Notice Of Hearing On Demurrer To Second Amended Complaint And Notice Of Motion To Strike Of Defts David Driver, Emilia Rallapalli, Krishn Rallapalli And Phillpine Rallapalli
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, August 11, 2009, line 5, DEFENDANT DAVID DRIVER, and, EMELIA RALLAPALLI, Demurrer To Second Amended Complaint and Defendants David Driver, Emilia Rallapalli, Krishn Rallapalli and Phillpine Rallapalli Motion to Strike. Demurrer sustained in part and overruled in part. Demurrer sustained without leave to amend as to Plaintiff Hussein regarding the sixth cause of action for trespass. Hussein fails to plead trespass damaging an ownership interest as described in Hassolt vs. Patrick Media Group (2000) 84 Cal.App. 4th 153, 171. No reasonable probability that defect can be cured by amendment. Demurrer overruled as to remaining causes of action. Hussein has standing to sue as he is not bringing an action against the HOA to enforce the CC&Rs, but rather is asserting claims against the individual defendants that arrose when he owned the property. Motion to strike denied, ten days leave to answer. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: LUIS ALJEJO et al VS. JACK O'CONNELL et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO PETITION FOR WRIT OF MANDATE/ PROHIBITION/ CERTIFICATION
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, August 11, 2009, line 8, RESPONDENT JACK O'CONNELL, STATE BOARD OF EDUCATION, SOPHIA ANGELIS, JAMES ASCHWANDEN, RAE BELISLE, ALAN BERSIN, RUTH BLOOM, YVONNE CHAN, GREGORY JONES, DAVID LOPEZ, JORGE LOPEZ, THEODORE MITCHELL, JOHNATHAN WILLIAMS, CALIFORNIA DEPARTMENT OF EDUCATION, THE STATE OF CALIFORNIA DEMURRER TO PETITION FOR WRIT OF MANDATE/ PROHIBITION/ CERTIFICATION. Demurrer sustained without leave to amend as to the State and the individual members of the State Board of Education. (See State of California vs. Superior Court (1974) 12 Cal.3d 237, 255-256.) The Court reads the petition liberally (CCP section 452.) Petitioner alleged no monitoring is taking place. Education Code section 52177 appears to be enforceable and requires "monitoring." Plaintiff properly plead standing under CCP section 526a, taxpayer standing for illegal expenditure/diversion of funds. Cause of action for declaratory relief appropriate. (CCP section 1060.) Ten days leave to answer. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: MARVA TAYLOR VS. CHARLES EDEN et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiff Marva Taylor'S Motion For Summary Judgment For Possession
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, August 11, 2009, line 11, Plaintiff Marva Taylor's Motion for Summary Judgement. Granted. No opposition filed. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: ALBERT BARTAL VS. MIKE PRYZVEIZSKI et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Demurrer And Demurrer Of Deft To Pltf'S Amended Complaint;
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, August 11, 2009, line 10, DEFENDANT MIKE PRYZVEIZSKI Demurrer To Plaintiff's Amended Complaint. Demurrer overruled in part, sustained in part. Notices state cure or quit in the alternative and are sufficient. However, Notice to pay or quit indicates rent due from May 1, 2008 to April 30, 2009. CCP section 1161(2) required the 3-day notice to pay rent or quit must be served within "one year after the rent becomes due." Strict compliance required. Motion to strike granted as to the 3-day notice to pay rent or quit. Five days leave to answer. =(302/CWW)
